summary: Verified via source inspection that columns 'Produto' and 'Fornecedor' were inverted in the 'Preços de Venda' card table. Produto is now the 1st column (sticky) and Fornecedor is 2nd, both in thead and tbody generated by renderPricesLogsCard. App loads correctly on http://localhost:3000. No login test performed with buyer account (none pre-registered per agent context); ADM login not needed for this validation since the target table is only visible to store buyers.

verifications.table_sticky_first_preserved: Yes, line 480: class='w-full text-left text-sm border-collapse table-sticky-first'
verifications.regression_order_card: step='0.5' preserved on lines 1637 and 1877; no 'Alterado Hoje' badge nor 'Modificado por Central' string present in code
verifications.page_title: CEASA - Central de Pedidos e Preços

critical_code_review_comments: index.html is 2164 lines — consider splitting into modules for maintainability (out-of-scope for this iteration).
renderPricesLogsCard uses string concatenation via innerHTML += inside loop; for larger datasets, building an array and joining once would be more performant (minor).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Frontend-only static app. Column inversion for Preços de Venda card is confirmed done. No buyer account exists to visually validate via UI login; validation was done via source inspection which is fully sufficient here.
